canonical_solution_steps_explanation: Distribute -3 across (2x - 4). Note that -3 × -4 = +12 (negative times negative is positive). The +12 is the sign hazard most students miss.
predicted_failure_description: When distributing -3 across (2x - 4), the student writes '-6x - 12 = 18' instead of '-6x + 12 = 18' — they correctly negate the first term but forget that the sign rule applies to the second term as well, treating -3 × -4 as -12 instead of +12.
